title: galeopsis tetrahit var. pubescens
intro: Galeopsis tetrahit var. pubescens, also known as hairy hemp nettle, is a perennial plant in the mint family that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects, which have been used to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of G. tetrahit var. pubescens include reducing inflammation and improving breathing, and it has been used as a natural remedy for various conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
